expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: blind scenario drafting captures prompt handling, template save, report builder loading, and visible report title outcomes
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Case focus coverage (required)”, it explicitly lists the four required focus areas and maps each to adjacent issues: “Prompt handling”, “Template save”, “Report builder loading”, and “Visible report title outcomes”. The Phase 4a Scenario Draft then includes subcategories and scenarios for each: “Workstation report authoring… prompts…”, “Save / Template behavior”, “Report Builder loading / editor initialization”, and “Window/report title and localization-visible outcomes”.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md declares “Primary phase under test — Phase: phase4a” and includes a “Phase 4a contract alignment (required)” section stating subcategory-only requirements. The produced draft is structured as subcategory-first (e.g., “Save / Template behavior”, “Report Builder loading / editor initialization”) with scenario → atomic action chain → observable verification leaves, and it avoids forbidden top-level canonical categories (no Security/i18n/EndToEnd as top-level headings).
